ogTitle: Piara Waters vs Piccadilly: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Piara Waters and Piccadilly.
heading: Piara Waters vs Piccadilly — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Piara Waters, Western Australia and Piccadilly, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Piccadilly has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Piara Waters (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Piara Waters or Piccadilly safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Piara Waters has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Piccadilly scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Piara Waters scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Piccadilly scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Piara Waters or Piccadilly?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Piccadilly has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
